recommendedMutationPrimitives.rationale: Most failures spend turns in date picker setup. Build Google Flights /travel/flights/search or /travel/explore URLs from parsed route, dates, trip type, cabin, passengers, and stops when task parameters are explicit.
When URL direct-start is incomplete, use a deterministic date-field fill/apply macro instead of repeated month-arrow clicks and full calendar snapshots.
Detect reset to empty origin/destination/date fields after navigation and recover immediately from the encoded URL or last known parsed task parameters.
Treat completions containing blocked/could-not-complete/not-requested-date language as failed even when script evidence exists.
Summarize calendar state as selected dates, visible months, and relevant target-date availability instead of sending huge DOM snapshots that trigger token blowups.

cases.goal: Search a one-way filght from Dublin To Athens Greece for 1 Adult that leaves on December 30 and analyse the price graph for the next 2 months.
cases.verdict: Google Flights search is already set to a one-way flight for 1 adult from Dublin to Athens on Wed, Dec 30 at https://www.google.com/travel/flights/search?tfs=CBwQAhonEgoyMDI2LTEyLTMwagwIAxIIL20vMDJjZnRyCwgDEgcvbS8wbjJ6QAFIAXABggELCP___________wGYAQI . Visible page state shows: 'One way', passenger count '1', origin 'Dublin', destination 'Athens', and departure 'Wed, Dec 30'. Visible top-flight evidence includes 'From 258 US dollars' with British Airways and, in the price-history area, a visible option 'From 212 US dollars' for Dec 30. In the open Price insights > Price graph dialog, the tooltip over the selected bar reads 'One-way trip', 'Wed, Dec 30', 'From $212'. The graph spans January 2027 and February 2027. Analysis of the visible 2-month graph: prices are highest around late December/very early January, with multiple bars roughly in the $170-$300 range and one peak near $300. Through most of January, prices flatten lower, mostly around about $100-$140, with only an occasional spike near about $180-$190. In early February there is another notable spike around about $250, after which prices mostly return to roughly $100-$160 for the rest of February. Overall, the graph suggests Dec 30 at $212 is higher than many January/February dates, while the cheapest-looking period over the next two months is much of mid-to-late January and several stretches of February at about $100-$120.

   Text shown: "From 934 US dollars... 1 stop flight with ANA... Total duration 15 hr 5 min" and "Carbon emissions estimate: 652 kilograms. +20% emissions."

6) Air Canada — $1,004 — Nonstop — 10 hr 15 min — 489 kg CO2e (-10%)
   Text shown: "From 1004 US dollars. Nonstop flight with Air Canada... Total duration 10 hr 15 min" and "Carbon emissions estimate: 489 kilograms. -10% emissions."

   Text shown: "From 1475 US dollars... 1 stop flight with EVA Air... Total duration 13 hr 25 min" and "Carbon emissions estimate: 814 kilograms. +49% emissions."

CO2 comparison (lowest to highest):
- Air Canada nonstop: 489 kg CO2e
- Korean Air: 530 kg CO2e

- ANA: 652 kg CO2e
- Cathay Pacific: 681 kg CO2e

cases.goal: Browse destinations on the Google Flights homepage from Seattle, look at destinations on a map, and recommend some famous places to travel that are within a reasonable distance and price.
cases.verdict:
Browsed Google Flights Explore from Seattle and reviewed destination prices directly on the map and left-side results list.

Evidence:
- URL: https://www.google.com/travel/explore?tfs=CBwQAxoOagwIAxIIL20vMGQ5anIaDnIMCAMSCC9tLzBkOWpyQAFIAXACggELCP___________wGYAQGyAQQYASAB&tfu=GgA
- Visible state: origin field shows 'Seattle'
- Visible map/list prices include: Las Vegas $96, Denver $98, San Diego $175, Los Angeles $180, San Francisco $189, Portland $197, New York $267, Washington, D.C. $218, New Orleans $217, Miami $255, Chicago $250, Toronto $280, Boston $303.

Recommendations for famous places within a reasonable distance and price from Seattle:
1. Las Vegas — $96, nonstop, 2 hr 39 min. Best budget pick and very easy trip.
2. Denver — $98, nonstop, 3 hr 4 min. Cheap and relatively short; great city + mountain access.
3. San Diego — $175. Good value for a popular coastal destination and still fairly close.
4. San Francisco — $189, nonstop, 2 hr 10 min. Famous city, short flight, reasonable fare.
5. Washington, D.C. — $218. Strong value for an iconic destination, though longer than West Coast trips.
6. New Orleans — $217. Good price for a famous culture/food destination with moderate travel time.

Best overall balance of distance and price:
- Best cheapest: Las Vegas, Denver
- Best famous West Coast city: San Francisco
- Best warm-weather value: San Diego
- Best farther iconic city under about $220: Washington, D.C. or New Orleans

Less attractive from the current map/list because of either higher price or longer travel for the value shown:
- New York $267
- Miami $255
- Boston $303
- Toronto $280

If choosing 3 top recommendations from this screen, I’d suggest: Las Vegas, Denver, and San Francisco.
